title: Sempervivella alba
intro: Sempervivella alba, also known as White Hens and Chicks, is a perennial succulent plant species in the family Crassulaceae. This plant has been traditionally used to treat various health issues, including skin conditions, inflammation, and wound healing, due to its antimicrobial and anti-inflammatory properties. The bioactive constituents of Sempervivella alba include flavonoids, saponins, and triterpenoids, which contribute to its medicinal effects. The most common herbal preparations made with Sempervivella alba include tinctures, infusions, and topical creams, which are used to soothe skin irritations, promote wound healing, and support overall health and well-being.
taxonomy: Sempervivella alba is a species within the genus Sempervivella. It belongs to the family Crassulaceae, which consists of flowering plants. The family Crassulaceae is further classified under the order Saxifragales, and then the class Magnoliopsida. Sempervivella alba is also classified as a succulent plant. This classification places it within the broader plant kingdom.
